title: FAMa Airstrike Destroys IS-Sahel Base Near Ménaka, ~50 Militants Neutralized
detail: Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) conducted an airstrike targeting an Islamic State – Wilayat Sahel (IS-Sahel / ISGS) encampment approximately 38 km northwest of Ménaka city in eastern Mali. FAMa claimed 50+ militants neutralized, with an entire terrorist base including fuel depots, vehicles, and logistics infrastructure destroyed. This was the third FAMa engagement in the Ménaka region within one week. A separate concurrent raid in the Inatabakat area of northern Ménaka reportedly eliminated the high-value target Abou Alghabhas Ichmali. Pro-junta sources reported former pro-government MSA Tuareg fighters integrated into FAMa participated. Independent verification of casualty figures is not available; FAMa claims have historically been inflated.

title: ECOWAS Appoints Kouyaté as Chief Negotiator to Reopen Dialogue with AES
detail: ECOWAS formally appointed former Guinean Prime Minister Lansana Kouyaté as its chief negotiator to reopen diplomatic dialogue with the Alliance of Sahel States (AES — Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger). Previous ECOWAS mediators — Senegal's President Faye (July 2024) and Ghana's President Mahama (March 2025) — both failed to break through the impasse. The appointment signals continued international efforts to prevent full diplomatic rupture between the three junta-led AES states and the broader West African regional bloc. The AES states withdrew from ECOWAS in January 2025 but informal contacts have continued.
